issues.title: Cross-feature import: cart application imports from products domain
issues.description: The cart feature's application layer directly imports `Product` type from the products feature's domain layer (`@/features/products/domain/types`). Features must NOT import directly from other features. This creates a bidirectional coupling between cart and products.
issues.fix: Move the shared `Product` type (or a CartProduct DTO) to `apps/store/src/shared/domain/types.ts` so both features can depend on a shared abstraction rather than on each other. Alternatively, pass `Product` as a parameter through shared interfaces.

issues.title: App Router page contains business logic (auth check + conditional render)
issues.description: The route file directly calls `createServerSupabaseClient()`, checks `supabase.auth.getUser()`, and conditionally renders two different feature pages based on authentication state. Route files should only import and render a single feature page; business logic and auth checks belong in the feature's application layer.
issues.fix: Create a dedicated feature page (e.g., `apps/auth/src/features/auth/presentation/pages/AuthIndexPage.tsx`) that handles the auth check and conditional rendering. The route file should simply render that page: `return <AuthIndexPage />`.

issues.title: App Router index page renders inline UI instead of delegating to a feature page
issues.description: The payments app index route (`/payments`) renders a full UI layout inline (main container, heading, i18n calls) without a corresponding feature page. Route files should be thin wrappers that only import and render a feature page.
issues.fix: Create `apps/payments/src/features/home/presentation/pages/PaymentsHomePage.tsx` (or similar) and move the JSX and `getTranslations` call there. The route becomes: `return <PaymentsHomePage />`.

issues.title: App Router index page renders inline UI instead of delegating to a feature page
issues.description: Same pattern as ARCH-003: the playground index route renders UI inline. Playground has relaxed quality expectations (see monorepo-architecture.md), so this is a warning rather than critical, but it is still a violation of the app-router thin wrapper rule.
issues.fix: Move the inline JSX to a feature page component, e.g., `apps/playground/src/features/home/presentation/pages/PlaygroundHomePage.tsx`.

issues.title: Cross-feature import in products presentation: uses cart hook directly
issues.description: ProductCard (in the `products` feature) imports `useAddToCart` from the `cart` feature (`@/features/cart`). HeroSection.tsx and MobileBarWithCart.tsx also do the same. Features should not import directly from each other; cross-feature communication should go through shared hooks, events, or context.
issues.fix: Lift `useAddToCart` to a shared application hook (e.g., `apps/store/src/shared/application/hooks/useAddToCart.ts`) or pass the handler as a prop injected at the page level. The `Product` type dependency can also be resolved by moving it to shared domain.

issues.title: Layer-level barrel exports (index.ts) are absent in almost all features
issues.description: Of all feature layers across all apps, only `apps/store/src/features/cart/domain/index.ts` and `apps/store/src/features/products/domain/index.ts` exist as layer-level barrel exports. All other feature layers (domain, application, infrastructure, presentation) across admin, auth, landing, payments, store, studio are missing `index.ts` files. The architecture rule recommends each layer has an `index.ts` exposing its public API.
issues.fix: Incrementally add `index.ts` barrel files to each layer as features are modified. Priority: layers most frequently imported (domain and application layers first). This does not need to be done all at once.

issues.title: Inline return type in utility function instead of named type
issues.description: The helper function `getErrorMessages` has inline return type `{ field: string; message: string }[]`. Per the No Hardcoding rule, non-trivial inline types in function signatures should be extracted to named types in the domain layer.
issues.fix: Define `interface FormError { field: string; message: string }` in `apps/studio/src/features/products/domain/types.ts` (or a local types file) and use `FormError[]` as the return type.

issues.title: Hardcoded fallback URL in next.config.ts
issues.description: The line `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL || 'http://localhost:54321'` hardcodes a localhost URL as a fallback. While this is in a config file (not app source), consistency with other apps (which do not have this fallback) would improve portability. The other apps' next.config.ts files omit this pattern.
issues.fix: Remove the hardcoded fallback and handle missing env variable gracefully, or document it as an intentional development default in a comment.

patterns.pattern: Feature barrel (index.ts) at feature root but not at layer level
patterns.description: All features have a root `index.ts` that exports the feature's public API, but no layer-level barrel exports. This is an incomplete application of the barrel export pattern. Direct internal cross-layer paths are used (e.g., `@/features/auth/presentation/pages/LoginPage`) rather than `@/features/auth`.
patterns.recommendation: Consider adopting layer-level barrels as features grow, or document that the current project standard is feature-root barrels only.

patterns.pattern: Application layer imports directly from infrastructure (dependency inversion)
patterns.description: Application hooks (useAddToCart, useProfile, usePaymentMethods, etc.) import directly from infrastructure files (`@/features/X/infrastructure/Y`). This is a common pattern here — the application layer DOES depend on the infrastructure concretions rather than injecting them. While this works for React Query (which acts as the abstraction), it technically violates the DIP.
patterns.recommendation: This is a widely consistent pattern across all apps and is pragmatic for React Query usage. Low-risk. If strict DIP is required, introduce repository interfaces in the domain layer.
